paper_title: Grazing and Pollination Effects on Seed Production

methods.description: This method describes the setup and execution of grazing experiments to study the effects of different grazing intensities on vegetation and seed production in a desert steppe environment.

steps.description: Grazing experiments have been carried out in the study area since 2013. This study was performed from May 2019 to October 2020.
steps.searchable_summary: set up grazing experiments in study area from 2019 to 2020

steps.description: We also calculated the monthly rainfall in 2019 and 2020 in the study area.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate monthly rainfall for 2019 and 2020

steps.description: According to the grazing capacity of desert steppe in Inner Mongolia, we selected three grazing intensities: control plot (no grazing), moderate grazing plot (two sheep per plot) and heavy grazing plot (four sheep per plot).
steps.searchable_summary: select grazing intensities based on desert steppe capacity

steps.description: The plots were all approximately four ha and the study plots were protected from other human impacts.
steps.searchable_summary: ensure plots are 4 ha and protected from human impacts

steps.description: There were three replicates (plots) for each grazing intensity and the experimental layout comprised a total of nine grazing plots.
steps.searchable_summary: create three replicates for each grazing intensity

steps.description: The sheep freely grazed in the enclosed fences in the day time and they were raised in the sheep pens under the same conditions at night.
steps.searchable_summary: manage sheep grazing and housing conditions

steps.description: We selected two-year old sheep with similar weight and healthy for the grazing experiments.
steps.searchable_summary: select healthy two-year old sheep for experiments

steps.description: In each plot, we set up five 2 m × 2 m quadrats at the center and the four corners respectively.
steps.searchable_summary: set up quadrats in each plot

steps.description: Vegetation measurements were conducted in August, when the standing aboveground biomass reached the peak.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct vegetation measurements in August

steps.description: For each quadrat, we used the projection method to measure the vegetation cover (VC) and then harvested aboveground biomass (AGB) of each species.
steps.searchable_summary: measure vegetation cover and harvest biomass

steps.description: We have dried samples in an oven using 65℃ for 48 h to constant mass and weighed.
steps.searchable_summary: dry and weigh biomass samples

methods.description: This method outlines the statistical analyses performed to assess the effects of pollination treatments, grazing intensities, and years on seed production, vegetation cover, and aboveground plant biomass.

steps.description: A generalized linear model (GLM) with a gamma distribution and logit link function was used to assess the effects of pollination treatments (open-pollinated and hand-pollinated), different grazing intensities (control, moderate and heavy grazing), and years (from 2019 to 2020) on seed production.
steps.searchable_summary: use GLM to assess effects on seed production

steps.description: We considered tag number as a random factor within the different grazing intensities.
steps.searchable_summary: include tag number as random factor in GLM

steps.description: We considered pollination treatments, grazing intensities and years as fixed factors, and seed set was the dependent variable in the model.
steps.searchable_summary: set fixed factors and dependent variable in GLM

steps.description: We used a likelihood ratio test to determine the variations in different grazing intensities and applied Tukey's method to adjust for multiple comparisons.
steps.searchable_summary: perform likelihood ratio test and Tukey's adjustment

steps.description: GLM was used to determine whether grazing intensity and year affected vegetation cover and aboveground plant biomass.
steps.searchable_summary: use GLM to assess effects on vegetation cover and biomass

steps.description: We used one-way ANOVA to compare the number of open flowers and pollinators.
steps.searchable_summary: perform one-way ANOVA on flower and pollinator numbers

steps.description: In addition, we used one-way ANOVA to test multi-group comparisons of the means and post hoc contrasts were performed using the S-N-K test.
steps.searchable_summary: perform one-way ANOVA and S-N-K test for multi-group comparisons

steps.description: We used regression to evaluate the relationship between the mean seed production of marked flowers and pollinator visitation frequency, with pollinator visitation frequency as the independent variable and mean seed production among labeled flowers as the dependent variable.
steps.searchable_summary: perform regression analysis on seed production and pollinator frequency

steps.description: We used SPSS 22.0 to perform these analyses.
steps.searchable_summary: use SPSS 22.0 for statistical analyses
